english: — rgya-nón (rgya non) W. the great royal seal, of a square form; surpassing (nón-pa (non pa)) all others in influence and power. — rgya-dpé (rgya dpe) a Sanskrit book Tar. 33, 2. — rgya-ṕi-liṅ (rgya phyi gling) n. of the country, rgya-ṕi-liṅ-pa (rgya phyi gling pa), n. of the people, through which the Tibetans heard first (prob. at the beginning of the eighteenth century) of the civilized nations of the occident, hence n. for British India, for Englishman, or European resident of British India, and also (sometimes without rgya (rgya)) for Europe and European in general. The word is of course not to be found in literature. Some derive it from 'Feringhi', which term, in the slightly altered form of ṕa-ráṅ (pha rang), ṕe-ráṅ (phe rang), is current in C., along with the above mentioned rgya-ṕi-liṅ (rgya phyi gling); it is therefore not improbable, that ṕi-liṅ (phyi gling) represents only the more vulgar pronunciation of the genuine Tibetan word ṕyi-gliṅ (phyi gling), an out-country, a distant foreign country and esp. Europe, Chr. Prot. — rgya-ṕib(s) (rgya phibs), rgya-ṕub(s) (rgya phubs) a Chinese roof Glr. — rgyá-ba (rgya ba) 1. vb. to be wide, extensive, pf. rgyas (rgyas) (q.v.), 2. sbst. width, extent, 3. adj. wide; rgyá-bar gyúr-ba (rgya bar gyur ba) to extend, to increase, to become copious Cs., perh. no longer in use. — rgyá-bọ (rgya bo) 1. Cs. and Lex. beard. 2. a Chinese Glr., but not without an allusion to the former signification. — rgya-dbáṅ rin-po-čé (rgya dbang rin po che) title of the Dalai Lama, v. Huc II., 275, where 'kian ngan' stands erroneously. — rgyá-ma (rgya ma) 1. a large steel-yard C., rgyá-ma-la dégs-pa (rgya ma la 'degs pa) to weigh Glr., *gyá-ma-la tég-nẹ* (*rgya ma la theg ne*) C. being weighed out by retail, e.g. meat; *gyá-ma-la ma tég-nẹ* (*rgya ma la ma theg ne*) C. wholesale. 2. a sealed paper, document. — rgya-mí (rgya mi) a Chinese. — rgyá-mo (rgya mo) 1. net Cs. 2. a Chinese woman Glr.; rgyá-mo-bza (rgya mo bza) id. — rgya-rmá (rgya rma) the venereal disease Sch. — rgya-smúg (rgya smug) violet colour C. — rgya-tsá (rgya tsha) sal ammoniac Med. — rgya-tsós (rgya tshos) Med., perh. = rgya-skyégs (rgya skyegs): — rgyá-mtsho (rgya mtsho) 1. sea, ocean; rgyá-mtshor jùg-pa (rgya mtshor 'jug pa) to go to sea Dzl., gtóṅ-ba (gtong ba) to let one go to sea Dzl.; ṕyii rgyá-mtsho (phyi'i rgya mtsho) the outer sea, ocean; náṅ-gi rgyá-mtsho (nang gi rgya mtsho) an inner sea, inland sea, lake. 2. Bal. (*rgyám-tso* (*rgyam mtsho*)) river. 3. dropsy Mṅg. 4. symb. num.: four. — rgya-gżi (rgya gzhi) W. is stated to be a kind of gtór-ma (gtor ma). — rgya-zór (rgya zor) reaping-hook, sickle. — rgya-gzéb (rgya gzeb) Sch. 'a large net', C. a large rake, used in reaping. — rgya-yúl (rgya yul) 1. a large country 2. China Glr. — rgyá-ra (rgya ra), rgyá-ru (rgya ru), occasionally rgya (rgya) alone, the Saiga-antelope Sch. — rgya-rí (rgya ri) a portion of meat, (= sder-gáṅ (sder gang) a plateful) small or large, Pth., W. C.; it also denotes a measure = ½ dum (dum), or ¼ lhu (lhu). — rgya-róg (rgya rog) beard C. — rgya-láb (rgya lab) talk, gossip. — rgya-lám (rgya lam), high-road, high-way. — rgya-śóg (rgya shog) Chinese paper. — rgya-sér (rgya ser) 1. gap, cleft, fissure, chasm, in rocks, glaciers etc., 2. a dog with yellow spots about the nose Cs. 3. Russia, rgya-sér-pa (rgya ser pa) a Russian; cf. rgya-gár (rgya gar). — rgya-sóg (rgya sog) 1. W. a saw 2. Sch.: 'a Chuichui, or Chuichur, an infidel, a Mahometan; also Turkestan'. — rgya-sráṅ (rgya srang) the open street (opp. to house) Glr.

another form for rgyáb-pa (rgyab pa), used esp. in C., to throw, cast, fling, mda rgyág-pa (mda' rgyag pa) to shoot arrows Glr.; brág-la ču rgyág-pai ṕraṅ (brag la chu rgyag pa'i 'phrang) a path along a precipice, where the water rushes against the rock, i.e. where there is a cataract Glr.; dgón-pa żig rgyág-pa (dgon pa zhig rgyag pa) to found a monastery (= débs-pa ('debs pa)) Georgi Alph. Tib.; *gó-la zug gyag* (*go la zug rgyag*) C. = mgó-nad odebs (mgo nad 'debs).

provisions, victuals, food, in full: otshó-bai rgyags ('tsho ba'i rgyags), otsho-rgyags ('tsho rgyags); lam-rgyágs (lam rgyags) provisions for a journey; dgun-rgyags (dgun rgyags) prov. for the winter; rgyágs-ṕye (rgyags phye), provisions of meal or flour; rgyags-zóṅ (rgyags zong) merchandize to buy or barter victuals with.
fat, stout, Schr. also mighty, powerful, proud; rgyags-ṕrug (rgyags phrug) Pth., Schr.: bastard-child.
(so pronounced in Bal.) instead of gyaṅ (gyang), wall,
Pth.: rgyáṅ-nas bżag (rgyang nas bzhag) they laid him down with his hands and feet stretched out.
distance 1. absolutely: rgyáṅ(-ma)-nas (rgyang (ma) nas) at a distance, from afar, e.g. to see, to call to; rgyáṅ (-ma) (rgyang (ma))
